title: != vs !==
subtitle: Checking inequality
goal: Understand the difference between != and !== in JavaScript.
explanation: != checks whether values are different after possible type conversion.
!== checks whether values or types differ without automatic conversion.
Because of type conversion, != can give unexpected results.
For example, 0 != false returns false because JavaScript treats them as equal after conversion.
In most cases, it is safer to use !==.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: != is a soft check: it may adjust values first. !== is a strict check: if types differ, the values are already considered different.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often expect 0 != false to be true. But because of type conversion, JavaScript treats them as equal.

quiz.question: Which operator checks inequality without type conversion?
